description:
## Background and intent

This epic is the first user-visible Issue Explorer slice for Beadsmith, a desktop client for browsing and managing Beadwork projects without becoming a second issue tracker. It turns the starter Tauri app into a real, dense, three-pane issue explorer that reads All Issues from the Current Workspace and renders them through the project-standard integration stack.

The plan intentionally preserves the full feature intent while splitting work into independently landable beads. It does not collapse backend parsing, RPC setup, frontend state, layout, and user-level verification into one oversized task. The split exists so future agents can work safely in parallel, test each boundary, and avoid confusing raw Beadwork JSON with Beadsmith's frontend contract.

## Architectural decisions this epic follows

- ADR-0001: Beadsmith is a Tauri + React desktop app; local system access goes through explicit Rust/Tauri functionality.
- ADR-0002: Beadwork remains the source of truth. Beadsmith may reshape data for UI, but it must not become a second issue store.
- ADR-0003: Integrate through structured `bw` CLI output, not direct Beadwork git storage or human-rendered prompt/TTY text.
- ADR-0004: Use TauRPC for typed Tauri RPC and Effect for frontend async/service code. Do not introduce raw Tauri `invoke` as the app pattern for this functionality.

## Child tasks

- `bsm-8ul` - Add the pure Rust `bw list --all --json` adapter for the Issue List.
- `bsm-tes` - Build the static three-pane issue explorer shell from the mockup.
- `bsm-mq4.1` - Add reference-only TauRPC and Effect source under `docs/research/infra/`.
- `bsm-mq4.2` - Expose issue summaries through the typed TauRPC boundary.
- `bsm-mq4.3` - Add the Effect service/client for loading issue summaries.
- `bsm-mq4.4` - Render real `IssueSummary` rows in the issue explorer shell.
- Final smoke/e2e bead - Prove the complete Issue List slice works from a deterministic Beadwork workspace.

## Dependency order

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[bsm-8ul: Pure Rust bw adapter] --> T[bsm-mq4.2: TauRPC boundary]
    R[bsm-mq4.1: Reference source] --> T
    T --> E[bsm-mq4.3: Effect service]
    S[bsm-tes: Static shell] --> V[bsm-mq4.4: Render real rows]
    E --> V
    V --> Q[Final smoke/e2e coverage]
```

The Rust adapter, static shell, and reference-source checkout can proceed independently. The TauRPC boundary needs both the adapter and reference source. The Effect service needs the typed RPC contract. Rendering real rows needs both the static shell and service. The final smoke/e2e bead waits for the real rendering path and proves the whole user path.

## User-visible scope

Included in this epic:

- Launch Beadsmith into the issue explorer shell.
- Read All Issues from the Current Workspace using `bw list --all --json`.
- Display the Current Workspace path read-only.
- Render real issue summaries in the middle pane.
- Keep loading, true empty, and error states visible while preserving the app shell.
- Keep search/sidebar filters and detail pane visible but inert/empty where functionality is intentionally not implemented yet.

Not included in this epic:

- Workspace switching or saved workspace settings.
- Search/filter behavior.
- Issue selection, issue detail loading, editing, comments, attachments, or mutations.
- Local caching, local databases, or a second issue store.
- Direct reads from Beadwork git internals.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The Rust adapter lands independently of Tauri/TauRPC wiring.
- [ ] The static shell lands independently of backend data loading.
- [ ] TauRPC and Effect are adopted as the standard integration path for frontend-to-Rust app functionality.
- [ ] The final slice lists real Beadwork issues across stored statuses, not just actionable/default `bw list` output.
- [ ] The final UI preserves loading, empty, and error states while keeping the shell mounted.
- [ ] Focused tests cover adapter parsing/errors, RPC contract shape, Effect state mapping, and rendering states.
- [ ] A final smoke/e2e path proves the complete user-facing Issue List flow.
- [ ] Each child bead closes with the commands/checks it ran and any deferred limitations in a `bw comment`.

## Triage

ready-for-agent
title: Epic: List all issues in the issue explorer
